text: As artificial intelligence becomes more common in daily life, questions about ethics are increasingly important. One major concern is bias in AI systems. When trained on data that reflects past inequalities, an AI can make unfair decisions, for example in hiring or loan applications. This raises the issue of fairness: how do we ensure that AI treats everyone equally? Privacy is also essential, as many systems collect large amounts of personal data. To address these problems, transparency is needed so that people can understand how and why an AI makes its choices. Ultimately, responsible AI development requires careful planning, ethical guidelines, and ongoing oversight to protect individuals and society.
